title: romantic earthy polish scallions pilaf
description: a hearty scallion-based pilaf that combines earthy flavors and crunchy textures, perfect for a romantic polish dinner. scallions add a snappy pop of freshness to the dish, while a hint of shio-cured fennel adds a touch of sophistication.

ingredients.name: scallions
ingredients.notes: chop the scallions into 1-inch pieces, leaving some of the green intact for garnish.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use kosher or sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: chop the parsley into small pieces.

ingredients.name: crushed ayurvedic tamasic-friendly almonds
ingredients.notes: toast the almonds in a dry skillet over medium heat until fragrant.

instructions: heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. add the onion and saut49 until softened, about 5 minutes.
add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
add the caraway seeds and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
add the scallions and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they start to soften.
add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
gradually add the broth, whisking constantly to prevent lumps.
bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes.
stir in the shio-cured fennel and cook for 1 minute.
season the pilaf with salt and pepper to taste.
serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley and toasted almonds.
